source: iConnections Global Alts 2024 — Industry titans panel: AI, growth/crossover investing
summary: Open source LLMs will be a major winner; frontier model companies raised at $5-15B are likely zeros or sold at preference
summary_extended: Baker argues that open source large language models — specifically citing Meta's Llama and the startup Mistral — will be major winners in the AI landscape, effectively commoditizing frontier model capabilities. His core reasoning is that frontier model startups which raised at $5-15B valuations are structurally squeezed: on one side by hyperscalers (Google, Microsoft, Amazon) who have massive distribution advantages, and on the other by high-quality open source models that undercut their pricing. Baker uses pointed language — 'you're lucky if you get sold for the preference' — to convey that these startups will likely return only liquidation preference to investors, making them effective zeros for equity holders. The investment implication is to avoid private frontier model companies at current valuations, and to favor Meta as the primary public beneficiary given its Llama open source strategy positions it as the dominant open source AI platform while simultaneously pressuring competitors.
detail: Baker argues open source LLMs (citing Llama, Mistral) will win, and that frontier model companies that raised at $5-15B valuations will be lucky to be sold for their liquidation preference, squeezed between hyperscalers with distribution and high-quality open source.
confidence_evidence: Strong directional language ('you're lucky if you get sold for the preference'), specific valuation range cited ($5-15B), names specific models
quote_fragment: lucky if you get sold for the preference

ticker_audit.reasoning: Meta is the developer of Llama, the open source LLM Baker explicitly cites as a winner. Meta directly benefits from open source LLM adoption as it validates their strategy and strengthens their AI ecosystem positioning. This is the only US-listed public equity clearly implied by the thesis.
